description: List all tasks for the current user with optional filtering and sorting

changes: Added sort_by parameter for sorting tasks
Added sort_direction parameter for sort order control
Maintains backward compatibility (defaults to created_at desc)

completed.description: Filter by completion status. If not specified, returns all tasks (completed and incomplete).

sort_by.description: Field to sort tasks by. Defaults to 'created_at' if not specified.

sort_direction.description:
Sort direction. If not specified, defaults based on sort_by field:
- created_at: desc (newest first)
- due_date: asc (earliest first)
- priority: asc (high to low)
- title: asc (A-Z)
Note: For priority, 'asc' means high→medium→low (importance order), 'desc' means low→medium→high.

tasks.description: List of tasks sorted according to parameters

natural_language_examples.user_input: sort my tasks by due date
natural_language_examples.intent: Sort tasks with earliest due dates first
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Tasks with due dates appear in chronological order (earliest first), tasks without due dates appear at the end

natural_language_examples.intent: Sort tasks by importance (high to low)
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: High priority tasks first, then medium, then low. Within same priority, newest tasks first.

natural_language_examples.intent: Show newest tasks at the top
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Most recently created tasks appear first (default sort)
Tasks sorted A-Z case-insensitively ('attend meeting' before 'Buy groceries')

natural_language_examples.intent: See tasks with earliest due dates
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Tasks sorted with soonest due dates first

natural_language_examples.intent: Filter by status and priority, no specific sort
natural_language_examples.note: Priority filtering not yet implemented (Phase V Intermediate feature), but sort still works
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: All incomplete tasks shown, sorted newest first (default)

natural_language_examples.note: Agent needs to track current sort state and reverse sort_direction
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: If currently asc → switch to desc, if desc → switch to asc

error_handling.trigger: sort_by value not in [due_date, priority, created_at, title]

response.message: sort_by must be one of: due_date, priority, created_at, title

error_handling.trigger: sort_direction value not in [asc, desc, null]

response.message: sort_direction must be one of: asc, desc, or null (for default)
Failed to retrieve tasks. Please try again.

performance_requirements.implementation_approach: Database-level ORDER BY with composite indexes

security_requirements: User isolation: Only return tasks belonging to authenticated user

backward_compatibility.v1_behavior: If sort_by and sort_direction not specified, behaves identically to v1.0.0 (created_at desc)
backward_compatibility.breaking_changes: None - all new parameters are optional with sensible defaults
backward_compatibility.migration_path: No migration needed - existing clients continue to work

understanding_sort_commands: User says 'sort by X' → set sort_by to X
User says 'by importance' / 'by priority' → sort_by=priority, sort_direction=asc
User says 'reverse' / 'flip order' → toggle current sort_direction
default_behaviors: If user doesn't mention sort, use created_at desc (newest first)
If user says 'due date' without direction, use asc (earliest first)
If user says 'priority' without direction, use asc (high→medium→low)
If user says 'title' without direction, use asc (A-Z)
response_formatting: Always mention applied sort in response: 'Here are your tasks sorted by due date (earliest first):'
If tasks have no due dates, explain: 'Tasks without due dates appear at the end'
If many tasks have same priority, mention: 'Tasks with the same priority are sorted by creation date (newest first)'

test_cases.expected_sort: due_date ASC NULLS LAST, created_at DESC
test_cases.expected_behavior: Tasks with earliest due dates first, nulls last

test_cases.test_name: Sort by priority high to low
test_cases.expected_sort: CASE priority WHEN 'high' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 WHEN 'low' THEN 3 END ASC, created_at DESC
test_cases.expected_behavior: High priority tasks first, then medium, then low

test_cases.test_name: Sort with filter (completed tasks by due date)
test_cases.expected_behavior: Only completed tasks shown, sorted by due date

test_cases.test_name: Sort direction defaults based on field

modifications: Add sort_by parameter to function signature
Add sort_direction parameter to function signature
Add input validation for sort parameters
Implement SQL ORDER BY logic for each sort field
Handle NULL values with NULLS LAST
Add priority CASE statement for custom enum ordering
Add LOWER() for case-insensitive title sorting
Update agent prompt with sort examples
Add natural language patterns for sort commands
Include sort behavior explanations in system prompt
